completed_tasks.name: Phase 2.5 brainstorm: 3 waves of name candidates explored, web-checked for namespace conflicts
completed_tasks.progress: Brainstormed Hush/Whisp/Pith/Magpie/Cricket/Pip/Koe/Hayai/Bankai etc. Most have direct dictation-tool competitors. Cricket/Patter/Hayai/Bankai are clean. Discovered Koe (github.com/missuo/koe) is exact same architecture as voice-cc — paused for strategic decision.

remaining_tasks.name: Decide path forward: (A) keep building voice-cc, (B) pivot to thin wrapper around Koe's Rust core, (C) adopt Koe + archive voice-cc
If path A: pick name from clean candidates (Cricket, Patter, Hayai, Bankai) or generate another wave
Decide rename scope: binary name (voice-cc-record), Lua module name (require('voice-cc')), XDG paths (~/.config/voice-cc/)
Decide BRD-03 visual identity: pursue menu-bar glyph/icon now, punt to Phase 4, or skip
Decide tagline / positioning line for README + setup.sh banner

blockers.description: Discovered Koe (github.com/missuo/koe, 329 stars, MIT, March 2026) — same product as voice-cc but more polished: streaming ASR, LLM correction, multi-backend (Apple Speech / MLX Qwen3-ASR / sherpa-onnx / Doubao cloud), Fn-key default, Homebrew-distributed, 15MB native ObjC+Rust app. Question is whether to continue voice-cc or adopt/wrap Koe.
blockers.workaround: User paused to evaluate. Re-read .planning/phases/02.5-branding/.continue-here.md (Koe comparison section) before resuming.

human_actions_pending.action: Strategic decision: voice-cc vs Koe vs hybrid
human_actions_pending.context: Koe ships everything voice-cc would ship in Phases 3-5 (distribution, streaming, LLM correction). User needs to weigh: pure-local-by-default + hackability + sunk cost vs. adopt-and-move-on.

decisions.decision: Roadmap reordered: Phase 3 (Distribution + Public Install + hyperfine) moved to end of v1 — execute order is now 1 → 2 → 2.5 → 3.5 → 4 → 3 → 5
decisions.rationale: Distribute the polished product, not bare hardening. Phase 3.5 dep flipped from Phase 3 to Phase 2.5.

decisions.decision: Phase 2 hardening: three coupled Sequoia regressions fixed inline during live walkthrough — (a) SOX_SIGNALED flag distinguishes trap-induced sox exit from real failure, (b) amplitude-based TCC detection replaces dead stderr-fingerprint (Sequoia feeds silent stream not stderr error), (c) deep-link URLs use '://' for hs.urlevent.openURL
decisions.rationale: Plan 02-01 RESEARCH §4 was wrong about Sequoia 15.7.5 behaviour; spike worked only because wait || true masked exit codes

decisions.decision: Project paused: ~/.hammerspoon/init.lua require('voice-cc') and require('hs.ipc') commented out; Hammerspoon quit. Easy to re-enable (uncomment two lines).
decisions.rationale: User asked to stop everything running while evaluating Koe alternative

uncommitted_files: /Users/oliverallen/.hammerspoon/init.lua (user's personal Hammerspoon config — NOT in repo; commented out voice-cc requires)
next_action: Read .planning/phases/02.5-branding/.continue-here.md (Koe comparison section). User decides path forward (A/B/C). If A: resume Phase 2.5 discussion with name pick from clean candidates. If B: rescope Phase 2.5 + Phase 3 around Koe wrapping. If C: archive project with milestone summary.
context_notes: Phase 2 is closed (4/4 plans, all signed off, all 12 v1 requirements implemented). The strategic question landed via the Phase 2.5 namespace search: when checking Koe for availability, we found it's literally voice-cc but better — same press-and-hold-mac-dictation architecture, even uses Fn key (which user asked about during Phase 2 wrap-up). User has invested 7 plans of work but hasn't shipped. The honest read: Koe is more capable than voice-cc-as-designed. Build vs adopt is a vision call, not engineering.
